createidlx.gui.display_link.placeholders:
Use '$' or '{}' placeholders to insert the data
once or multiple times anywhere inside the string;

for displaying the above characters literally, use
backslash escaping: '\$' and '\{}' respectively.

In case of no unescaped placeholders present,
default concatenation method will be used instead.
Placeholders are disabled by the server config.

'Progress Bar' display format support
is %s by the server config.
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.header: Using Display Links' Attached Label and Placeholders
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_1: By default, the information string provided to the Display Link gets passed to the target display as is
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_2: Open the Interface to set an Attached Label text in its respective field
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_4: The inserted text will be displayed before the information string and separated from it by a space
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_5: Use placeholders ('$' or '{}') to mark the place, where the information string should be embedded, inside the label text
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_7: Utilizing multiple placeholders in one string is possible, and mixing both types doesn't affect the final result
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_9: If a literal '$' character has to be inserted (same applies to '{}'), without it turning into a placeholder, use backslash-escaping on it
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_11: As long as there are no unescaped placeholders present in the label altogether, default concatenation by a space will be applied
createidlx.ponder.attached_label.text_13: Hover over the Placeholders Usage Guide button in the Interface to see a tooltip briefly explaining placeholders
